system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.
user_prompt:
<similar_case_reference><index>
case name: obliqueShock
case domain: compressible
case category: None
case solver: rhoCentralFoam
</index>
<directory_structure>
<dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [T, U, p]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [momentumTransport, physicalProperties]</dir>
</directory_structure></similar_case_reference>
<similar_case_advice>match_level='high' use_scope='All configuration files from the obliqueShock reference case can be used directly as templates, including blockMeshDict (adjust vertices to 4.1×1.0×0.1 m), 0/ boundary conditions (set inlet U=2.9 m/s, top U=(3.0,-0.50632,0.0), T_inlet=1.0 K, T_top=1.25853 K, p_inlet=1.0 Pa), thermophysicalProperties (normalized molWeight=11640.3, Cp=2.5, gamma=1.4), fvSchemes, fvSolution, and controlDict (deltaT=0.0025 s, writeInterval=1.0 s, endTime=10 s).' advice='Use the obliqueShock tutorial case as a direct template since all settings—including solver, domain type, normalized thermodynamic properties, boundary condition structure, and time-control parameters—are an exact match; only minor adjustments to mesh dimensions and numerical values should be needed.'</similar_case_advice>
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    (4.1 1.0 0.1)
    (0   1.0 0.1)
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (164 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(

        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)

    }
    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    bottom

        (

        );
    }

        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         10;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   1.0;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

mixture

        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoCentralFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
inconsistent patch and patchField types for 
    patch type symmetryPlane and patchField type zeroGradient

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/obliqueShock/2/0/T/boundaryField/bottom from line 37 to line 37.

    From function static Foam::tmp<Foam::fvPatchField<Type> > Foam::fvPatchField<Type>::New(const Foam::fvPatch&, const Foam::DimensionedField<Type, Foam::volMesh>&, const Foam::dictionary&) [with Type = double]
    in file /global/cfs/cdirs/m4756/theseus/OpenFOAM-10/src/finiteVolume/lnInclude/fvPatchFieldNew.C at line 154.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<user_requirement>do a 2D oblique shock wave simulation using OpenFOAM with the rhoCentralFoam solver. The computational domain spans 4.1 m x 1.0 m x 0.1 m. Use inlet velocity of 2.9 m/s and top velocity of (3.0,-0.50632,0.0) m/s. Use an inlet temperature of  1.0 K and top boundary temperature of 1.25853 K, and an inlet pressure of 1.0 Pa. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use a time step of 0.0025 seconds, output results every 1.0 seconds, and run the simulation until a final time of 10 seconds.</user_requirement>
Please review the error logs and provide guidance on how to resolve the reported errors. Make sure your suggestions adhere to user requirements and do not contradict it.
response:
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
inconsistent patch and patchField types for 
    patch type symmetryPlane and patchField type zeroGradient
```

The `bottom` boundary is defined as `symmetryPlane` in `blockMeshDict`, but in the `0/T` file, the `bottom` boundary condition is set to `zeroGradient`. OpenFOAM requires that when a patch is declared as `symmetryPlane` type, the corresponding boundary condition in all field files must also be `symmetryPlane`.

## Resolution

**In the `0/T` file**, change the `bottom` boundary condition from `zeroGradient` to `symmetryPlane`:

Currently it reads:
```
bottom
{
    type            zeroGradient;
}
```

It should be changed to:
```
bottom
{
    type            symmetryPlane;
}
```

This makes the `bottom` boundary condition consistent across all field files (`U`, `T`, `p`) with the `symmetryPlane` patch type declared in `blockMeshDict`.

Additionally, reviewing the `p` file, the `outlet` uses `extrapolatedCalculated`, which may cause issues depending on the OpenFOAM version being used. For consistency with the reference obliqueShock case and to avoid potential future errors, consider changing the `outlet` boundary condition in `p` from `extrapolatedCalculated` to `inletOutlet` (matching what's used in `U` and `T`), though this is not the current error source.

**Summary of required changes:**
1. **`0/T`**: Change `bottom` from `zeroGradient` to `symmetryPlane`
2. All other field files already have consistent boundary conditions and do not need modification.
